src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is defined in java.io.Serial; without the import, the code will not compile.

src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.general_idea: Replace manual index-based access to the last element of a List with the Java 21 List.getLast() method.

src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 17+ serialization best practices enforced by stricter compilers or linters in the migration path.
src/main/java/org/springframework/sample.why_exists: Compilation failure or warning treated as error due to missing @Serial annotation on serialVersionUID in Java 17+ environments where strict serialization checks are enabled.
